outcome_text: Potential benefits of intended functionality are examined and documented.
scoring_text: Ability to assess and document potential benefits of AI system functionality, including conducting benefit analysis, identifying value propositions for stakeholders, and documenting expected positive outcomes from AI system deployment.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to assess and document potential benefits of AI system functionality, including conducting benefit analysis, identifying value propositions for stakeholders, and documenting expected positive outcomes from AI system deployment.
reference_document_element_description: Execute bias audits on AI systems in development and production, documenting methodology, findings, and recommended interventions.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-3.1 (MAP) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-T-009 is the strongest match (STS: 0.589, cosine: 0.696). Secondary: RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.688), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.686).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.589). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documenting]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, analysis, assess, benefit, benefits, conducting] vs WIDAI terms [audits, bias, development, execute, findings, interventions] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.887, R=0.895). Balanced P/R — the 29-word competency implication and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (91.3%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.696 > STS 0.589 (gap +0.107). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, documenting] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, documenting]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, analysis, assess, benefit, benefits, conducting]. WIDAI-only: [audits, bias, development, execute, findings, interventions].
